name: Amplitude, Inc.

profile.description: Amplitude is a leading digital analytics platform that helps companies understand user behavior and optimize product experiences. The company serves over 4,797 paying customers including 27 Fortune 100 companies, with $366M in annual recurring revenue growing 17% year-over-year.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Amplitude is at the forefront of AI-driven analytics with its launch of agentic AI agents including Global Agent, AI Feedback, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration. AI agents now drive 25% of platform queries. The company expanded integrations with Anthropic, OpenAI, Cursor, Figma, and GitHub. Enterprise and multi-product customers account for 74% of ARR, and $100K+ customers grew 18% YoY.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: CEO Spenser Skates has articulated a clear vision around AI as a structural tailwind for digital analytics. The company demonstrates strong product velocity and has attracted over 25 AI-native companies as $100K+ customers. However, the company remains unprofitable with 'stubbornly low' margins as noted by analysts, which may affect employee morale.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: FY2025 revenue was $343.2M (+17% YoY) with Q4 revenue of $91.4M. ARR reached $366M and remaining performance obligations grew 35% to $417.7M. However, the company is still posting sizable losses with a profit margin of -25.8% and ROE of -32.7%. At $7.01 per share with forward P/E of 40.9x, the stock is expensive relative to its growth rate and losses.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI is a clear tailwind for Amplitude. The company is embedding AI directly into its product with agentic analytics, and AI agents already drive 25% of platform queries. CEO Skates stated 'AI is a structural tailwind for Amplitude.' The rise of AI-native companies creates new demand for product analytics. Rather than being disrupted by AI, Amplitude is leveraging it to accelerate growth and differentiate its platform.

infinite_game.summary: Amplitude shows infinite-game thinking through its mission to help companies build better products through data-driven insights and its aggressive AI investment. However, persistent losses and pressure to demonstrate profitability create finite-game tensions.

just_cause.analysis: Amplitude's mission to 'help companies build better products' through behavioral analytics serves a genuine need for data-driven product development. The company's platform enables more personalized, effective digital experiences for billions of end users.

trusting_teams.analysis: The company has approximately 700 employees and has maintained strong product velocity with rapid AI feature releases. However, margin pressure and the need for cost discipline may create internal tensions between growth investment and profitability targets.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Amplitude competes against Mixpanel, Google Analytics, and Heap (now part of Contentsquare). The company differentiates through enterprise capabilities and AI-first analytics, and its 27 Fortune 100 customers validate competitive strength. ARR growth acceleration for six consecutive quarters shows competitive momentum.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Amplitude has evolved from a pure analytics platform to an AI-powered product intelligence suite. The introduction of agentic AI agents, MCP integrations, and AI Feedback demonstrates willingness to reimagine its product around emerging technology paradigms.

courage_to_lead.analysis: The company has taken a bold stance on AI-native analytics and invested heavily in agentic capabilities. However, persistent losses and the $100M buyback authorization suggest competing pressures between growth investment and shareholder returns.

investment_thesis: Amplitude is a leading digital analytics platform with AI as a structural tailwind. ARR growth has accelerated for six consecutive quarters to 17.3%, remaining performance obligations grew 35% to $417.7M, and the company is guiding to 15% revenue growth in FY2026 ($390-398M). AI agents driving 25% of queries represent a differentiated product advantage.
thesis_continued: The company remains unprofitable with -25.8% profit margins and -32.7% ROE. Forward P/E of 40.9x is expensive for a company not yet profitable. Analysts note margin levels have been 'stubbornly low' and look like they will remain low for another year. Competition from Google Analytics, Mixpanel, and Contentsquare/Heap is intensifying.
key_catalysts: AI agents driving 25% of platform queries with agentic analytics positioning Amplitude as an AI-first analytics leader
Remaining performance obligations of $417.7M (+35% YoY) providing strong forward revenue visibility
Enterprise momentum with $100K+ customers growing 18% YoY and 27 Fortune 100 companies as customers
key_risks: Persistent unprofitability with -25.8% net margin and no clear timeline to breakeven despite $343M in revenue
Forward P/E of 40.9x is expensive for a growth-stage company with decelerating revenue growth (17% vs 25% 5yr CAGR)
Competition intensifying from Google Analytics (free tier), Mixpanel, and Contentsquare's Heap acquisition
